error.signature: AI misreads the Schengen 90/180 rule as 90 days per country, or as resettable by a quick exit to the UK

verdict.summary: Schengen's 90/180 rule caps short-stay entrants at 90 days within any rolling 180-day window across the ENTIRE Schengen area (27 countries including Germany, France, Italy, Spain, etc.), not per country. Exiting to a non-Schengen country (UK, Ireland, Croatia until 2023, Turkey) pauses the clock but the accumulated days do not reset.

dead_ends.action: Tell a traveler they can stay 90 days in Germany, then 90 in France, then 90 in Italy
dead_ends.why_fails: Schengen treats the bloc as one jurisdiction for short-stay purposes (Regulation (EU) 2016/399 Art. 6). Days accumulate across every Schengen country entered. Border guards run the 90/180 calculator on exit; overstay is detected immediately.
dead_ends.condition: Trying to treat Schengen countries as separate quotas

dead_ends.action: Exit Schengen to London for 2 days to 'reset' the 90/180 counter
dead_ends.why_fails: Time outside Schengen pauses but does not reset the rolling counter. The calculator looks at any 180-day window: if you've spent 90 days inside in the last 180, re-entry is refused until enough days have 'aged off'.

dead_ends.action: Extend a Schengen visa-waiver stay by applying to a German Ausländerbehörde for an extension
dead_ends.why_fails: Extensions of short-stay visa-waiver status are granted only in exceptional cases (force majeure, humanitarian, late entry into force). A routine tourist request to extend by another month is almost always denied.

workarounds.action: Use the official EU short-stay calculator before travel. Plan the itinerary so cumulative Schengen days ≤ 90 in any 180-day window.
For stays >90 days, apply for a national long-stay D-visa from the German consulate abroad (Freelancer, Job-seeker, Student, Working Holiday for eligible nationalities). Process takes 4–12 weeks.
Alternate Schengen stays with extended trips to non-Schengen countries (UK, Ireland, Turkey, Morocco, Balkans) so that by re-entry the 180-day window has rotated past the earlier Schengen days.
workarounds.condition: Flexible itinerary, full understanding of rolling window
workarounds.tradeoff: Requires careful date tracking; a miscalculation is a visa overstay with Schengen-wide ban.
